findings.title: Batched ETH commitments can reuse one `msg.value` multiple times

best_agent_match.title: Batched ETH bids can reuse a single `msg.value` to mint multiple commitments

findings.title: Anyone can front-run initialization of an uninitialized auction and seize admin/proceeds control

best_agent_match.title: Any first caller can initialize an uninitialized auction and seize admin and wallet control

findings.title: The auction accounts for nominal transfer amounts instead of actual tokens received

best_agent_match.title: The auction books nominal token amounts instead of actual received amounts

findings.title: Admin can redirect auction proceeds after users have already committed

best_agent_match.title: Arbitrary wallet change after auction commitments

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent use of nonReentrant modifier
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic pattern only; no concrete reentrant exploit is supported by the code, and the cited unguarded functions do not expose a realistic permissionless reentrancy path with protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in price calculation functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Initialized auctions enforce `_minimumPrice > 0` in both `initAuction()` and `setAuctionPrice()`, so `clearingPrice()` cannot be zero in the live configuration this contract permits.

rejected_candidates.title: Deprecated Solidity version 0.6.12
rejected_candidates.reason: Using an older compiler version is not, by itself, a concrete reportable vulnerability in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: Outdated `.transfer()` pattern for ETH transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited `_tokenPayment()` helper is unused; live ETH payouts use `_safeTransferETH`, and the direct `transfer` in `commitEth()` only refunds the caller-chosen beneficiary within the same transaction.

rejected_candidates.title: Comment and code discrepancy for finalizeTimeExpired
rejected_candidates.reason: Documentation mismatch only; it does not create a realistic exploit or protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Wallet address can trigger finalization
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing the configured payout wallet to finalize does not bypass the existing success/timeout conditions and does not by itself create a theft or insolvency vector.

rejected_candidates.title: IERC20 interface requires permit which many tokens lack
rejected_candidates.reason: `permitToken()` is an optional helper; calling it against a token without `permit()` only reverts that caller's own transaction and does not endanger auction funds or state.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing validation for pointList contract
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an admin-only misconfiguration risk; the admin already controls whether the point list is enabled, so no permissionless exploit or unexpected asset loss is shown.
